name: Internal Developer Platform
description: A unified internal platform that abstracts infrastructure complexity, enabling teams to self-serve environments, deployments, and observability.

name: AI-Assisted Code Review
description: Automated code review using LLMs to catch logic bugs, security issues, and style violations before human review.

name: Observability Stack (OTel + Grafana)
description: OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation with Grafana dashboards for traces, metrics, and logs across all services.

name: Feature Flag System
description: Centralized feature flag platform enabling gradual rollouts, A/B testing, and kill switches without deployments.

name: Event-Driven Architecture
description: Asynchronous communication between services via event streams, enabling loose coupling and real-time data flows.

name: WebAssembly on the Server
description: Running WebAssembly workloads on the server side for sandboxed, portable, high-performance computation.

name: Apache Kafka
description: Distributed event streaming platform used as the backbone for our event-driven architecture.

name: Backstage (Developer Portal)
description: Spotify's developer portal for cataloging services, documentation, and self-service actions in one place.

name: SST (Serverless Stack)
description: Framework for building serverless applications with TypeScript, offering live Lambda debugging and infrastructure as code.

name: Platform Engineering Team
description: A dedicated team building and maintaining the internal developer platform, treating infrastructure as a product.

name: DORA Metrics Dashboard
description: Automated tracking of Deployment Frequency, Lead Time, MTTR, and Change Failure Rate across all teams.

name: GraphQL Federation
description: We continue to see teams struggle with the operational complexity of Apollo Federation. Consider simpler alternatives.
